mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where unemployment crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant logit coefficient (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of periods where industrial production increases during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged sectors, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew when the rate threshold is breached, with mean returns exceeding zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between payroll growth and unemployment decline yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which systematically produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an information coefficient greater than 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below the 100 index baseline during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side weakness and deflationary pressure, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in more than 55% of observations where industrial production falls below the 100 threshold during recovery regimes.
